topic: NLP-Based Automation of Systematic Literature Reviews in Computer Science
rqs: What NLP automation tools improve recall and precision of systematic literature reviews for computer science researchers compared to manual screening?
Which transformer‑based summarizers achieve the highest F1‑score and time savings when extracting data from ACM Digital Library and IEEE Xplore?
In which contexts (e.g., conference proceedings vs. arXiv) do semantic search engines most enhance coverage and scalability of SLRs in computer science?
base_query: ("Computer Science Researchers" OR "Systematic Review Authors" OR "Software Engineers" OR "Data Mining Practitioners" OR "Machine Learning Researchers" OR "Academic Librarians" OR "Research Methodologists") AND ("NLP Automation Tools" OR "Deep Learning Models" OR "Transformer Based Summarizers" OR "Information Extraction Pipelines" OR "Topic Modeling Techniques" OR "Semantic Search Engines" OR "Citation Recommendation Systems") AND ("Manual Screening" OR "Rule Based Extraction" OR "Traditional Keyword Search" OR "Hybrid Human AI Approaches" OR "Baseline Statistical Methods" OR "Existing SLR Software" OR "Expert Judgment") AND ("Recall" OR "Precision" OR "F1-score" OR "Time Savings" OR "Coverage" OR "Automation Accuracy" OR "Scalability") AND ("ACM Digital Library" OR "IEEE Xplore" OR "ArXiv Preprints" OR "CS Conference Proceedings" OR "University Repositories" OR "Industry Technical Reports" OR "Open Source Code Repositories")
